description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, giving the block a distinctive, textured appearance. The stone has a dark, mottled coloration, suggesting it is made from a dense, durable material such as granite or basalt. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with no visible joints or separations, indicating it is a single, monolithic piece. The grooves are oriented lengthwise and are visible from two opposing sides, while the other two sides show only the solid face of the block. This type of block is commonly used as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments, such as on sidewalks, plazas, or as part of a traffic control system.
description: A dark, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The block is solid and monolithic, with smooth sides and a rugged, durable appearance.
description_view_0: The block is seen from the grooved side, showing the striped pattern running horizontally across the top surface.
description_view_1: This view highlights the depth and spacing of the grooves, which run vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, the grooves are visible running horizontally, but the lighting and angle emphasize the block's thickness.
description_view_3: The grooves are again prominent, running vertically, with the block's solid, smooth sides visible.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or decorative obstacle
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or spaced as a standalone barrier
